1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: WHEN THE WORLD is shaken up by its cataclysm,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: When the earth is shaken with its appointed tremor.

1.author: Arberry
1.text: When earth is shaken with a mighty shaking

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: When the earth is shaken by the shaking thereof,

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: When the earth is shaken with its (final) earthquake.

1.author: Itani
1.text: When the earth is shaken with its quake.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: When the earth will be shaken with a mighty shaking,

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: When the earth quakes with its Zilzal.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: When Earth is shaken with her (final) earthquake

1.author: Qarai
1.text: When the earth is rocked with a terrible quake

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: When the earth is shaken with its mighty shaking,

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: When the earth is shaken with its [final] earthquake

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: When the earth is shaken by a terrible quake

1.author: Shakir
1.text: When the earth is shaken with her (violent) shaking,

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: When the earth is shaken with its violent shaking,

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: When the earth is shaken to her (utmost) convulsion,

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: And the earth throws out its burdens,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: And the earth throws out its burdens.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: and earth brings forth her burdens,

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: And the earth bringeth forth her burthens,

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: And when the earth throws out its burdens,

2.author: Itani
2.text: And the earth brings out its loads.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: and the earth will throw up all her burdens,

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: And when the earth throws out its burdens.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: And Earth yieldeth up her burdens,

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: and when the earth brings forth its burdens

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: And the earth discharges its burdens

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: and it throws out its burden,

2.author: Shakir
2.text: And the earth brings forth her burdens,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: when the earth shakes off her burdens,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: And the earth throws up her burdens (from within),

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: And man enquires: "What has come over it?"

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And man says, “What has happened to it?”

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And man saith: what aileth her?

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And man will say: "What is the matter with it?"

3.author: Itani
3.text: And man says, “What is the matter with it?”

3.author: Maududi
3.text: and man will cry out: “What is the matter with her?”

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: And man will say: "What is the matter with it"

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: And man saith: What aileth her?

3.author: Qarai
3.text: and man says, ‘What is the matter with her?’

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: and the human asks: "What is the matter with it?"

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And man says, "What is [wrong] with it?" -

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: the human being will say (in horror), "What is happening to it?"

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And man says: What has befallen her?

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: when man asks, "What is happening to her?";

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: And man cries (distressed): 'What is the matter with her?'-

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: That day it will narrate its annals,

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: On that day earth will narrate its news,

4.author: Arberry
4.text: upon that day she shall tell her tidings

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: On that Day she will tell out the tidings thereof.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: That Day it will declare its information (about all what happened over it of good or evil).

4.author: Itani
4.text: On that Day, it will tell its tales.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: On that Day it will relate all her news,

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: That Day it will declare its information.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: That day she will relate her chronicles,

4.author: Qarai
4.text: On that day she will relate her chronicles

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: On that Day it shall proclaim its news,

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: That Day, it will report its news

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: On that day the earth will declare all (the activities of the human being) which have taken place on it,

4.author: Shakir
4.text: On that day she shall tell her news,

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: on that Day it will narrate its account,

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: On that Day will she declare her tidings:

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: For your Lord will have commanded it.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Because your Lord sent a command to it.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: for that her Lord has inspired her.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Because thy Lord will inspire her.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: Because your Lord has inspired it.

5.author: Itani
5.text: For your Lord will have inspired it.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: for your Lord will have commanded her (to do so).

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: Because your Lord will inspire it.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: for her Lord will have inspired her.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: for your Lord will have revealed to it.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Because your Lord has commanded it.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: having been inspired by your Lord.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Because your Lord had inspired her.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: for your Lord has so directed it.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: For that thy Lord will have given her inspiration.

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: That day people will proceed separately to be shown their deeds.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: On that day men will return towards their Lord, in different groups, in order to be shown their deeds.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Upon that day men shall issue in scatterings to see their works,

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: On that Day mankind will proceed in bands, that they may be shewn their works.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: That Day mankind will proceed in scattered groups that they may be shown their deeds.

6.author: Itani
6.text: On that Day, the people will emerge in droves, to be shown their works.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: On that Day people will go forth in varying states so that they be shown their deeds.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: That Day mankind will proceed in scattered groups that they may be shown their deeds.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: That day mankind will issue forth in scattered groups to be shown their deeds.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: On that day, mankind will issue forth in various groups to be shown their deeds.

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: On that Day mankind shall issue in scatterings to see their deeds.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: That Day, the people will depart separated [into categories] to be shown [the result of] their deeds.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: On that day, people will come out of their graves in different groups to see (the results of) their own deeds.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: On that day men shall come forth in sundry bodies that they may be shown their works.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: On that Day people will come forward in separate groups to be shown their deeds:

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: On that Day will men proceed in companies sorted out, to be shown the deeds that they (had done).

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Yawmaithin yasduru alnnasu ashtatan liyuraw aAAmalahum

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Whosoever has done even an atom's weight of good will behold it;

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: So whoever does a good deed equal to the weight of the minutest particle, will see it.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: and whoso has done an atom's weight of good shall see it,

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Then whosoever hath worked good of an atom's weight shall behold it

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: So whosoever does good equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant), shall see it.

7.author: Itani
7.text: Whoever has done an atom's weight of good will see it.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: So, whoever does an atom's weight of good shall see it;

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: So, whosoever does good equal to the weight of a speck of dust shall see it.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And whoso doeth good an atom's weight will see it then,

7.author: Qarai
7.text: So whoever does an atom’s weight of good will see it,

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: Whosoever has done an atom's weight of good shall see it,

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it,

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Whoever has done an atom's weight of good,

7.author: Shakir
7.text: So. he who has done an atom's weight of good shall see it

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: whoever has done the smallest particle of good will see it;

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Then shall anyone who has done an atom's weight of good, see it!

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Faman yaAAmal mithqala tharratin khayran yarahu

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: And whosoever has done even an atom's weight of evil will behold that.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And whoever does an evil deed equal to the weight of the minutest particle, will see it.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: and whoso has done an atom's weight of evil shall see it.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And whosoever hath worked ill Of an atom's weight shall behold it.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: And whosoever does evil equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant), shall see it.

8.author: Itani
8.text: And whoever has done an atom's weight of evil will see it.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: and whoever does an atom's weight of evil shall see it.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: And whosoever does evil equal to the weight of a speck of dust shall see it.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: And whoso doeth ill an atom's weight will see it then.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: and whoever does an atom’s weight of evil will see it.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: and whosoever has done an atom's weight of evil shall see it.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: will see it and whoever has done an atom's weight of evil, will also see it.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And he who has done an atom's weight of evil shall see it.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: while whoever has done the smallest particle of evil will see it.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: And anyone who has done an atom's weight of evil, shall see it.

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Waman yaAAmal mithqala tharratin sharran yarahu
